text:
ense poet Bhartrihari sings the praise and function
or wealth in ten verses. He points out to us how it is necessary for all
men to live a dignified life. It is wealth and money alone that enables us
to live a life of independence without appeal to others for our livelillood.
One is asked to adventure forth on the hi9h·seas for securing fortune.
If a man cannot support himself and his family on the material plane.
how couk:l he work for moksha whiCh is very difficult. Even those who
tal: of sanyasa (renunciation) must acquire something to give it up.
They can11ot renounce nothing; it is empty sanyasa, a mockery of the
concept.
The Hindu ethicists have insisted on the necessity of man to earn
his ivelihood by the sweat of his brow while he is fit. strong and young,
for achieving anything substantial in human life_. one must be free from
indigence and misery and enjoy a certain degree of economic
competence. He must seek as far as possible to be setf.sufficient.
Poet Nilak.
